hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When 'lifetime_ms' is set to 0, the entry has an infinite expiration window and will not be removed by an expiration check, effectively overriding any default global lifetime.
When reading an entry that exists but has expired, the system prints 'undefined' and immediately deletes the entry from the in-memory store, behaving as if the key were never present.

context_pointers.actual_meaning: Output must wrap the JavaScript object literal into a JSON string by enclosing the raw object in double curly braces, resulting in a string like '{"key":"value"}' rather than just the object.
If the serialized value contains a special marker key '$bytes_base64', the output string must explicitly include the prefix 'bytes:' followed immediately by the UTF-8 encoded base64 string.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_basic_storage.json.description: Values written under a logical key can be read back from the same cache namespace, and each successful write reports that the value was accepted.

feature2_removal_and_absence.json.description: A missing key reads as an undefined value; removing an existing key reports success and makes later reads undefined, while removing a missing key reports no removal.

feature3_clear_all_entries.json.description: Clearing a cache namespace removes every value in that namespace and reports an undefined result for the clearing operation.

feature4_expiration_rules.json.description: Stored values can expire after a default lifetime, per-entry lifetimes override the default, and an explicit zero lifetime keeps a value from expiring.

cases.expected_output:
saved=true
saved=true
saved=true
t0_foo=bar
t0_fizz=buzz
t0_ping=pong
t150_foo=bar
t150_fizz=undefined
t150_ping=pong
t250_foo=undefined
t250_ping=pong
t350_ping=undefined

feature5_namespace_isolation.json.description: Independent namespaces can use the same logical keys without collisions; removal or clearing in one namespace does not remove values in another namespace.

cases.expected_output:
saved=true
saved=true
saved=true
saved=true
clear_alpha=undefined
alpha_foo=undefined
alpha_bar=undefined
beta_foo=value-beta
beta_bar=value-beta

feature6_value_shapes.json.description: The cache preserves supported value shapes including booleans, nulls, numbers, objects, byte sequences, quoted strings, and large numeric values.

cases.expected_output:
false_value=false
null_value=null
number_value=0
object_value={"fizz":"buzz"}
bytes_value=bytes:YmFy
object_bytes_value={"buff":"bytes:YnV6eg=="}
quote_value="
large_number_value=9223372036854776000

feature7_raw_record_view.json.description: A caller may request the stored record shape instead of only the value, receiving the saved value together with its expiration metadata.

feature8_custom_codec.json.description: When custom encoding and decoding hooks are supplied, writes pass through the encoder and reads pass through the decoder while preserving the user-facing value.

feature9_uri_backed_storage.json.description: A file-backed storage URI can be used as a cache backend; values can be read after writing and are absent again after the namespace is cleared.

_constraints.output_rule: A line prefixed with 'saved=TRUE' must be printed immediately after the save action

_constraints.input_condition: Action is 'read' AND the key does not exist in the store (or is absent/removed)
_constraints.output_rule: A line must be printed with the provided 'label', setting the value to 'undefined'

_constraints.input_condition: Action is 'remove' AND the key exists in the store
_constraints.output_rule: A line must be printed with the provided 'label', setting the value to 'true'

_constraints.input_condition: Action is 'remove' AND the key does not exist in the store
_constraints.output_rule: A line must be printed with the provided 'label', setting the value to 'false'
A line must be printed with the provided 'label', setting the value to 'undefined'

_constraints.input_condition: Action is 'read' AND the entry exists but has expired
_constraints.output_rule: The system must treat the entry as absent: output 'undefined' and remove the entry from the in-memory store
The value must never expire; it remains valid even if global time advances beyond the default lifetime

_constraints.input_condition: Stored value is a JSON Object
_constraints.output_rule: Output format must wrap the printed object in curly braces: '{"key":"value"}'
Output format must prefix the base64 string with 'bytes:': 'bytes:{base64}'
Output line must strictly contain the word 'null' without quotes
Must output the raw value under 'record.value' and expiration metadata under 'record.expires'
Must output 'encoder_calls:{count}' and 'decoder_calls:{count}' reflecting the number of operations performed

_constraints.input_condition: 'key' field is present in a step
_constraints.output_rule: 'label' field is optional. If omitted, the output line uses the 'key' value. If present, output uses 'label'.

_constraints.input_condition: 'namespace' field is not provided in a step
_constraints.output_rule: The system must fallback to a default namespace (e.g., 'default' or 'keyv' based on context), but tests explicitly provide 'namespace': 'keyv'

_constraints.input_condition: Multiple namespaces exist. 'clear' is called on Namespace A
_constraints.output_rule: Values in Namespace B (using the same backend) must remain untouched and accessible
Subsequent 'read' actions evaluate expiration against the current time (simulated by accumulated milliseconds)

hide.oracle_response: When 'lifetime_ms' is set to 0, the entry has an infinite expiration window and will not be removed by an expiration check, effectively overriding any default global lifetime.
When reading an entry that exists but has expired, the system prints 'undefined' and immediately deletes the entry from the in-memory store, behaving as if the key were never present.

vague_refs.actual_meaning: Output must wrap the JavaScript object literal into a JSON string by enclosing the raw object in double curly braces, resulting in a string like '{"key":"value"}' rather than just the object.
If the serialized value contains a special marker key '$bytes_base64', the output string must explicitly include the prefix 'bytes:' followed immediately by the UTF-8 encoded base64 string.
